# data-chart — Full API Reference for AI Assistants

> This document provides exhaustive detail for AI assistants generating HTML with data-chart. data-chart is a zero-dependency library that converts HTML `<table>` elements into SVG charts using only data attributes.

## Overview

data-chart works as follows:

1. User includes a `<script>` tag and writes standard HTML `<table>` elements with `data-chart` attributes
2. On DOMContentLoaded, the library scans for `table[data-chart]` elements
3. Each table is parsed: thead → series names, tbody → categories and values
4. An SVG chart is rendered and inserted after the table
5. The table is hidden (accessible via screen readers)
6. MutationObserver watches for dynamically added tables

No JavaScript configuration objects. No `init()` call. No build step.

---

## Complete Attribute Reference

### `data-chart` (REQUIRED)

The chart type to render. Must be one of the 5 built-in types.

**Values:** `bar`, `line`, `area`, `pie`, `donut`

```html
<table data-chart="bar">...</table>
<table data-chart="line">...</table>
<table data-chart="area">...</table>
<table data-chart="pie">...</table>
<table data-chart="donut">...</table>
```

### `data-chart-height`

Chart height in pixels.

**Default:** `220`
**Example:** `data-chart-height="300"` → 300px tall chart

### `data-chart-colors`

Custom color palette as comma-separated hex values. If fewer colors than series/slices, the default palette fills in.

**Default palette:** `#2d5be3`, `#1a8c5a`, `#d94e3f`, `#c78a1e`, `#6b4ed4`, `#1a8c8c`, `#e05898`, `#4a90d9`

```html
<table data-chart="pie" data-chart-colors="#ff6384,#36a2eb,#ffce56">...</table>
```

### `data-chart-grid`

Grid line direction.

**Default:** `y`
**Values:**
- `y` — Horizontal grid lines only
- `x` — Vertical grid lines only
- `both` — Both directions
- `none` — No grid lines

**Note:** Grid lines are not rendered for pie/donut charts.

### `data-chart-legend`

Legend position.

**Default:** Auto — `top` if 2+ series, `none` if single series
**Values:**
- `top` — Legend above chart
- `bottom` — Legend below chart
- `none` — No legend

For pie/donut charts, legend shows slice labels with percentages.

### `data-chart-radius`

Corner radius for bar chart rectangles in pixels.

**Default:** `3`
**Example:** `data-chart-radius="0"` → sharp corners

### `data-chart-horizontal`

Boolean attribute (no value needed). Renders bar chart horizontally.

**Applies to:** `bar` type only

```html
<table data-chart="bar" data-chart-horizontal>...</table>
```

### `data-chart-stacked`

Boolean attribute. Renders bar chart with stacked series.

**Applies to:** `bar` type only

```html
<table data-chart="bar" data-chart-stacked>...</table>
```

**Can combine with horizontal:**
```html
<table data-chart="bar" data-chart-horizontal data-chart-stacked>...</table>
```

### `data-chart-animate`

Boolean attribute. Enables entrance animations.

**Default behavior without `animate`:** Chart renders instantly.

**With `animate`:**
- Bars: Height/width grows from 0
- Lines: Stroke draws left-to-right
- Dots: Fade in after line draws
- Areas: Opacity fades in after line
- Pie/Donut slices: Scale from center

Automatically respects `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce`.

### `data-chart-animate-duration`

Animation duration per element in milliseconds.

**Default:** `600`
**Example:** `data-chart-animate-duration="1000"` → 1 second

### `data-chart-animate-stagger`

Stagger delay between animated elements in milliseconds.

**Default:** `60`
**Example:** `data-chart-animate-stagger="100"` → 100ms between each bar/slice

---

## Table Data Format

### Basic Structure

```html
<table data-chart="bar">
  <caption>Chart Title</caption>
  <thead>
    <tr>
      <th></th>           <!-- Ignored (category column header) -->
      <th>Series A</th>   <!-- Series name -->
      <th>Series B</th>   <!-- Series name -->
    </tr>
  </thead>
  <tbody>
    <tr>
      <th>Jan</th>        <!-- Category label -->
      <td>100</td>        <!-- Value for Series A -->
      <td>80</td>         <!-- Value for Series B -->
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <th>Feb</th>
      <td>200</td>
      <td>150</td>
    </tr>
  </tbody>
</table>
```

### Rules

- `<tbody>` is required. Without it, the library throws `NO_DATA`.
- `<thead>` is optional. If absent, the first `<tbody>` row is used as header.
- `<caption>` is optional. Used as `aria-label` for the SVG.
- First cell in thead row is always ignored (it's the category column header).
- First cell in each tbody row is the category label.
- Remaining cells are numeric values.

### Value Parsing

The parser automatically handles formatted numbers:

| Input | Parsed As |
|-------|-----------|
| `100` | 100 |
| `1,234` | 1234 |
| `$99.99` | 99.99 |
| `¥1,200` | 1200 |
| `€50` | 50 |
| `£30` | 30 |
| `₩5000` | 5000 |
| `85%` | 85 |
| `-42` | -42 |
| `abc` | 0 |

Supported currency symbols (stripped from leading position): `¥`, `$`, `€`, `£`, `₩`

### Single Series vs Multi-Series

**Single series** (1 data column): No legend shown by default. For pie/donut, each row becomes a slice.

```html
<table data-chart="pie">
  <thead><tr><th></th><th>Share</th></tr></thead>
  <tbody>
    <tr><th>Chrome</th><td>65</td></tr>
    <tr><th>Firefox</th><td>15</td></tr>
    <tr><th>Safari</th><td>12</td></tr>
  </tbody>
</table>
```

**Multi-series** (2+ data columns): Legend shown at top by default. Each column is a separate series.

```html
<table data-chart="bar">
  <thead><tr><th></th><th>2024</th><th>2025</th></tr></thead>
  <tbody>
    <tr><th>Q1</th><td>300</td><td>450</td></tr>
    <tr><th>Q2</th><td>400</td><td>500</td></tr>
  </tbody>
</table>
```

---

## Chart Type Details

### Bar Chart (`data-chart="bar"`)

- Default: vertical bars, grouped by category
- `data-chart-horizontal`: flips to horizontal bars
- `data-chart-stacked`: stacks series on top of each other
- `data-chart-radius`: controls corner rounding (default 3px)
- Supports negative values
- Grid defaults to `y` (horizontal lines)

### Line Chart (`data-chart="line"`)

- Renders connected line paths with data point dots
- Each series is a separate line
- Dots are rendered at each data point
- Grid defaults to `y`

### Area Chart (`data-chart="area"`)

- Like line chart but with filled region below the line
- Area fill uses reduced opacity
- Line is rendered on top of the fill
- Grid defaults to `y`

### Pie Chart (`data-chart="pie"`)

- Circular chart with slices
- Uses first series data only (single column)
- Each row = one slice, sized by proportion
- Legend shows label + percentage
- No grid or axis labels
- Category labels from first column of tbody

### Donut Chart (`data-chart="donut"`)

- Same as pie but with center hole
- Displays total value in center
- Same data rules as pie

---

## Animation Details

When `data-chart-animate` is set:

| Element | Animation | Duration | Notes |
|---------|-----------|----------|-------|
| Bars (vertical) | Height grows from 0, y position adjusts | `animDuration` | Staggered per bar |
| Bars (horizontal) | Width grows from 0 | `animDuration` | Staggered per bar |
| Bars (stacked) | Each segment grows sequentially per column | `animDuration / 2` per segment | Column-then-segment order |
| Lines | Stroke draws left-to-right via dashoffset | `animDuration × 1.6` | No stagger |
| Dots | Fade in | 300ms | After line completes, staggered |
| Areas | Opacity fade in | 800ms | 400ms delay after line |
| Slices (pie/donut) | Scale from 0.3 to 1 + fade in | `animDuration` | Staggered per slice |

All animations disabled when `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce` is active.

---

## Theming

### Automatic Dark Mode

The library detects `prefers-color-scheme: dark` and adjusts colors:

| Property | Light | Dark |
|----------|-------|------|
| Text | `#4a4a44` | `#c8c8c0` |
| Muted text | `#8a8a80` | `#9a9a90` |
| Grid lines | `#e6e5df` | `#2c2c28` |
| Background | `#ffffff` | `#1a1a18` |

### CSS Custom Properties

- `--dc-font-family` — Font for labels and legends (default: `system-ui, -apple-system, sans-serif`)

### CSS Classes

| Class | Element |
|-------|---------|
| `.data-chart-container` | Wrapper div (table + SVG) |
| `.data-chart-svg` | The SVG element |
| `.data-chart-rendered` | Applied to converted tables |
| `.data-chart-grid` | Grid lines group |
| `.data-chart-axis` | Axis labels group |
| `.data-chart-series` | Series data group |
| `.data-chart-legend` | Legend group |
| `.data-chart-bar` | Individual bar rect |
| `.data-chart-line` | Line path |
| `.data-chart-area` | Area fill path |
| `.data-chart-dot` | Data point circle |
| `.data-chart-slice` | Pie/donut slice path |

---

## JavaScript API

```js
window.dataChart.init()          // Scan & render all table[data-chart]
window.dataChart.render(table)   // Render a single table element
window.dataChart.refresh(table)  // Destroy & re-render
window.dataChart.destroy(table?) // Remove chart(s), no arg = remove all
window.dataChart.version         // "0.1.0"
```

### Events

Dispatched on the `<table>` element:

```js
table.addEventListener('datachart:rendered', (e) => {
  console.log(e.detail.svg);  // SVGSVGElement
  console.log(e.detail.data); // ParsedData
});

table.addEventListener('datachart:error', (e) => {
  console.log(e.detail.message); // Error message
});

table.addEventListener('datachart:destroyed', () => {
  console.log('Chart removed');
});
```

### TypeScript Exports

```typescript
import type { DataChartType, DataChartGrid, DataChartLegend, DataChartAttributes } from 'data-chart';
import type { ParsedData, ChartType, ChartConfig, DataChartAPI } from 'data-chart';
```

`DataChartAttributes` extends React's `HTMLAttributes` for JSX autocomplete.

---

## Accessibility

- SVG gets `role="img"` and `aria-label` (from `<caption>` or auto-generated)
- Source table gets `aria-hidden="true"` (data is in the SVG)
- Table remains in DOM for screen readers and no-JS fallback
- Animations respect `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce`

## data-anim Integration

`data-anim-*` attributes on the table are forwarded to `.data-chart-container` for the data-anim animation library to process. data-anim is completely optional.

```html
<table data-chart="bar" data-anim="fadeInUp" data-anim-trigger="scroll">
  ...
</table>
```

## Anti-FOUC

An optional CSS file prevents flash of unstyled table content:

```html
<link rel="stylesheet" href="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/data-chart@0/dist/data-chart.css">
```

Or inline:
```css
table[data-chart] { visibility: hidden; position: absolute; width: 0; height: 0; overflow: hidden; }
```

---

## Best Practices for AI-Generated HTML

1. **Always include `<thead>`** for explicit series names
2. **Use `<caption>`** for descriptive chart titles
3. **Use `data-chart-animate`** for visual appeal on content pages
4. **Use `data-chart-colors`** to match brand colors
5. **Keep tables under 20 rows** for readable charts
6. **Use `data-chart-horizontal`** when category labels are long
7. **Use `data-chart-stacked`** to show part-of-whole relationships in bar charts
8. **Use `pie` or `donut`** only for showing proportions (keep slices under 7)
9. **Ensure table works without JS** — progressive enhancement is the core principle
